title: Fix percentage() scale (0–100)
body:
## Problem

In `percent_bug/stats.py`, `percentage(part, whole)` should return a value on the 0–100 scale.

`percentage(1, 4)` should equal `25.0`, but the implementation multiplies by `1` instead of `100`, so it returns `0.25`.

## Expected

`return (part / whole) * 100` when `whole != 0`. Keep raising `ZeroDivisionError` when whole is 0.

## Acceptance

All tests in `tests/test_stats.py` pass.
notes: Local arithmetic scale bug for offline eval.
